quizzes.lessonTitle: Drying failures, secondary damage and the mould rule
quizzes.title: Carpet Cleaning Technician Fundamentals — Knowledge Check

questions.questionText: Which carpet fibre is a natural protein fibre that is sensitive to high alkalinity, high heat and over-wetting?
options: Nylon
Polyester
Wool
Olefin (polypropylene)

questions.questionText: A carpet has a jute backing woven into its construction. What is the main risk if it is over-wet during cleaning?
options: The pile will become permanently glossy
The jute backing can brown and wick moisture badly
The fibre will change colour to white
Over-wetting has no effect on woven carpet

questions.questionText: Oily and greasy soil in carpet is best broken down before extraction by:
options: Cold water alone
A surfactant-based pre-spray matched to oily soil
Increasing vacuum-only dry time
A strongly acidic rinse applied first

questions.questionText: What is the correct practice regarding cleaning chemical products in carpet cleaning?
options: Mix two compatible-sounding brands for a stronger result
Always use the strongest available concentration
Never mix cleaning chemical products, and always check the current label and SDS before use
Mixing is safe as long as both products are alkaline

questions.questionText: Which cleaning method introduces the most moisture and therefore needs the most careful drying management?
options: Dry compound cleaning
Bonnet cleaning
Low-moisture encapsulation
Hot water extraction (steam)

questions.questionText: A commercial site needs a same-day appearance refresh on a low-pile carpet with minimal downtime. Which method best suits this situation?
options: Hot water extraction with maximum solution
Bonnet cleaning
A strongly alkaline soak with no extraction
Leaving the carpet to air-dry for two days before any work

questions.questionText: Before applying a cleaning product across a visible area of carpet, what should a technician always do first?
options: Apply it directly to the most visible area to check the result
Test it on a small, inconspicuous area for colourfastness and fibre reaction
Double the manufacturer’s recommended dilution
Skip testing if the fibre looks like nylon

questions.questionText: What is the correct order of the core cleaning process for carpet?
options: Extract, then vacuum, then pre-spray
Pre-spray, then vacuum, then groom, then extract
Vacuum, pre-spray and agitate, extract, then groom
Groom, agitate, vacuum, then pre-spray

questions.questionText: A standard Australian general power outlet (GPO) used to power drying equipment on a carpet cleaning job is rated at:
options: 10 A at 230 V
15 A at 230 V
20 A at 250 V
32 A at 400 V three-phase

questions.questionText: Visible mould is found in the underlay beneath a carpet being cleaned. What is the correct approach to drying?
options: Run air movers directly over the affected area to dry it as fast as possible
Do not run air movers across the affected area; contain, use AFDs and dehumidifiers first, and introduce air movers only after the area is cleaned and cleared
Increase airflow to blow the spores outside through an open window
Continue the normal drying plan unchanged
